scores.correctness_notes: Correctly identifies both events (certificate_available/certificate_denied match CertificateAvailableEvent/CertificateDeniedEvent). Adds extra details on renewal and key rotation. However, misses the gold standard's key point about certificate revocation by the provider and the request_errors mechanism with specific error codes (101-103, 201). The interface contract (CSR requirements, CA flag, databag) is not covered.
scores.hallucination_notes: Several method names (request_certificate_creation(), renew_certificate(), regenerate_private_key(), import_private_key()) and parameters (renewal_relative_time) appear to be from an older version of the tls-certificates library (charms.tls_certificates_interface.v3 era), not the new charmlibs-interfaces-tls-certificates~=1.0 API referenced in the gold standard. These methods exist in older library versions so not fully fabricated, but the 90% validity period renewal behaviour and specific method signatures may not apply to the new charmlibs API.
